remember: Students will be able to identify and locate India in the world map.
Students will be able to recall the geographical coordinates of India.
understand: Students will be able to explain the significance of India's location in the Asian continent.
Students will be able to describe the country's latitudinal and longitudinal extent.
apply: Students will be able to apply the concept of India's location to understand its climate, vegetation, and wildlife.
Students will be able to use the geographical location of India to analyze its trade and cultural relationships with other countries.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the impact of India's location on its economy, politics, and environment.
Students will be able to compare and contrast the geographical location of India with other countries in the region.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the location of India in the world map.
To identify the countries that share borders with India.
board_exam_objectives: What CBSE tests from this chapter is the ability to identify and locate India in the world map.
The board also tests the understanding of the geographical coordinates of India and its significance.
